comment: Every entry MUST carry a real citation. tolerance_ours=true marks numbers that are this project's tolerance/noise-floor choices layered on top of the cited observation - not values from the source itself. If a threshold cannot be sourced, it does not belong in this file.

tempo_ratio.source: Novosel & Garrity, Tour Tempo (Doubleday, 2004): tour-player video frame counts 18/6, 21/7, 24/8, 27/9 - all 3.0:1
tempo_ratio.notes: The 3:1 center is Novosel's published observation. The +/-0.5 band is this project's tolerance; the source reports elite clustering at 3:1 and amateurs commonly at 2:1 or below, not a cutoff.

downswing_duration.source: Novosel & Garrity, Tour Tempo (Doubleday, 2004): tour downswings of 6-9 frames at 30 fps
downswing_duration.notes: Range read directly from the published tour frame counts (6-9 frames at 30 fps = 0.20-0.30 s).

lead_knee_extension.source: Hume, Keogh & Reid, 'The Role of Biomechanics in Maximising Distance and Accuracy of Golf Shots', Sports Medicine 35(5), 2005: describes lead-leg extension ('posting up') through impact in skilled golfers
lead_knee_extension.notes: Directional rule only: our knee angle is a 2D projection (view-dependent), and a direction of change survives projection where a magnitude does not. The +5 deg minimum change is this project's noise floor, not from the source.

xfactor.source: McLean, 'Widen the Gap', Golf Magazine (1992); Myers et al., Journal of Sports Sciences 26(2), 2008: hip-shoulder separation around 45-50 deg in skilled golfers
xfactor.notes: Published values vary by measurement method, so the band is kept wide. Our depth-assisted estimate is approximate and normally fails the quality gate - this entry exists for when measurement improves.
